step_by_step_reasoning: The transcript describes a multi-tenant CI/CD pipeline. The developer triggers the workflow by committing code to AWS CodeCommit. AWS CodeBuild builds the application and outputs a Docker image to Amazon ECR. AWS CodePipeline orchestrates this flow, triggering AWS CloudFormation for deployment. CloudFormation executes a three-step safety check (ChangeSet, Approve, Execute) before provisioning and updating the architecture inside customer VPCs, including Amazon ECS tasks, an Application Load Balancer (ALB), and IAM roles.

graph.name: Automated Multi-Tenant Infrastructure Deployment with AWS CloudFormation
graph.notes: CI/CD pipeline managing deployments for over 100 customer environments concurrently using template-based CloudFormation deployments.

nodes.name: Developer
nodes.notes: Commits application code and infrastructure templates.

nodes.name: AWS CodeBuild
nodes.notes: Compiles code and builds Docker container images.

nodes.name: AWS CodePipeline
nodes.notes: Orchestrates continuous integration and continuous delivery stages.

nodes.name: AWS CloudFormation
nodes.notes: Provisions infrastructure using Change Sets, manual approval, and executions.

nodes.name: Application Load Balancer
nodes.notes: Distributes incoming traffic to ECS tasks.

nodes.name: AWS IAM
nodes.notes: Defines identity roles and permissions for AWS resources.
